cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source for this event is unclear, but may be related to a minor eruption centered near N05E30 starting round 2026-05-21T07:00Z seen in SDO AIA 304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: CME visible to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source is an M2.3 flare peaking at 2026-05-22T10:29Z from AR 14436 (approximately N18W96) as seen in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery. Dimming is best seen in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery. Opening field lines and post-eruptive arcades are visible in SDO AIA 171/193/284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement based on source location as well as the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT.
Preliminary measurement based on source location using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NW in GOES CCOR-1 starting around 2026-05-22T19:30Z. It is also seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO C3. It is missed in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 due to real-time data gaps. There is not a separate, front-sided source for this CME, so it is most likely far-sided and possibly outflow following earlier CME: 2026-05-22T10:48Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to lack of available coronagraph triangulation and lack of visible source candidate.

cmes.note: Narrow CME with clear front in STEREO A COR2. The front in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 that possibly corresponds to this event is very faint and unclear. No clear source has been found for this CME, although there is a far-sided field line movement around 2026-05-23T02:11Z pretty clearly in SUVI 284, but the CME does not triangulate to the east limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: A very speculative analysis based on the fit with STEREO A COR2 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3. While the COR2A front is bright, the front in LASCO is extremely faint. No source has been found on the earth-facing disk for this CME.

cmes.note: Faint loop CME to the E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs. No clear source for this CME is seen in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Very approximate analysis based on the fit of the loop fronts in SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs and not confirmed by the source location as no source has been found in EUV imagery.

cmes.note: Narrow faint CME seen to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and likely not seen in STEREO A COR2. No clear source for this CME has been found in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: It is not clear if the CME is seen in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph and no source has been found for it, so only plane-of-sky analysis can be done.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and as a faint N/NW partial halo in STEREO A COR2. The source is very likely former AR 14436, located around N18 and W100-W110. Observed as field line movement and loops breaking over the NW limb in SDO AIA 171/193 starting around 2026-05-23T05:18Z, and directly observed in STEREO A EUVI 195 as slow dimming and brightening starting around 2026-05-23T06:05Z in a wide area around former AR 14436's location. This active region is in close proximity to a large coronal hole to its southwest, possibly affecting CME propagation.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between the clear view in LASCO C3 and faint, difficult to resolve view in STEREO A COR2. Despite the source being located around W105, this front does not fit well outside of the W70-W80 range, with available imagery and its quality. Attempts to fit around W105 do not match the COR2A structure, but no other corresponding eruptions or fronts to account for this are identified, so it is possible this CME is just greatly deflected away from the source coordinates.

A preliminary SOHO LASCO C3 analysis close to the approximate observed source of the CME around N18W105. This does not match the STEREO A COR2 front for full triangulation (see other CME analysis note), but is presented due to the magnitude of the other analysis' deflection.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SE only in SOHO LASCO C2, better in running difference imagery, fading after a few early frames. The source is a rapid snap of field lines to the SE starting at 2026-05-23T07:19Z in GOES SUVI 284, from a likely far-sided source yet to rotate on.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only plane-of-sky.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SE only in SOHO LASCO C2, better in running difference imagery, fading after a few early frames. The source is a rapid snap of field lines to the SE starting at 2026-05-23T11:51Z in GOES SUVI 284, from a likely far-sided source yet to rotate on.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only plane-of-sky.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NNE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and due N in STEREO A COR2. Likely occulted by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. Source is far-sided over the NE limb, observed best in GOES SUVI 284 as a wide breaking of field lines over the NE starting around 2026-05-23T14:43Z as part of a series of similar field line movements and CME fronts at this time.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 triangulation.

cmes.note: CME observed to the WNW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and NW as a partial halo in STEREO A COR2. The source is just NW of AR 14441 (N08W67), centered around N18W75. First observed as field line movement and loop breaking on the NW limb in SDO AIA 171/193, and dimming in SDO AIA 193, starting around 2026-05-23T15:01Z; also observed well in STEREO A EUVI as a wide dimming Starting around 2026-05-23T16:05Z identifying AR 14441 as the source and not former AR 14436 located further behind the limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 triangulation.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NNE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and due N in STEREO A COR2. Likely occulted by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. Source is far-sided over the NE limb, observed best in GOES SUVI 284 as a wide breaking of field lines over the NE starting around 2026-05-23T16:15Z as part of a series of similar field line movements and CME fronts at this time.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 triangulation.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NNE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and due N in STEREO A COR2. Likely occulted by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. Source is far-sided over the NE limb, observed best in GOES SUVI 284 as a wide breaking of field lines over the NE starting around 2026-05-23T16:59Z as part of a series of similar field line movements and CME fronts at this time.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 triangulation.

cmes.note: Fast CME with a brighter, narrower bulk partially obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 and a fainter halo shock seen to the N in COR2A and to the NE in CCOR-1 (and initially covered by the scheduled data gap in SOHO LASCO). It is associated with the fast far-sided eruption seen to the NNE in SOHO LASCO 171 and GOES SUVI 284 as fast movement of field lines and restructuring of magnetic field and as long, narrow strands of a filament in GOES SUVI 304. It is associated with type II radio burst.

cmeAnalyses.note: Approximate analysis of the brighter bulk based on the fit with two coronagraphs, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Approximate analysis of the faint halo shock front based on the fit with two coronagraphs, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.

Preliminary, very approximate analysis of the brighter bulk based on the fit of early coronagraph imagery with two coronagraphs, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. This far-sided CME is partially obscured by the pylon in CCOR-1 making analysis difficult.

cmes.note: CME seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO and GOES CCOR-1 with an irregular front overlapping with earlier NW CMEs. The front is likely also seen to the NW in a couple of timestamps of STEREO A COR2. Its source is not clear: one source could be a minor eruption in AR 14441 (N8W68)seen at 2026-05-23T10:45 in STA EUV 304/195 seen as brightening and dimming but it is not confirmed by the fit with coronagraphs. Another source could be AR 14436 (N18W110) which has been showing signs of activity for many hours.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative, approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and on Active Region 14436 (N18W110) as a possible source.

cmes.note: CME seen to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2A, likely associated with a far-sided eruption as evidenced by moving field lines off the western limb seen best in SDO AIA 171 near 2026-05-24T09:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using all coronagraphs for triangulation of the longitude.

cmes.note: Narrow CME seen to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and to the north-northwest in STEREO A COR2A with an uncertain source, likely associated with far-sided eruptive activity as evidenced by the CME geometry.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement using all available spacecraft (SOHO, STEREO A, GOES) coronagraphs out to nine solar radii.

cmes.note: Minor, narrow CME seen to the east in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but not in STEREO A COR2A due to poor image quality. The CME is most likely associated with far-sided eruptive activity.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge plane of sky measurement as the source longitude is uncertain.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-05-24T20:00Z. It is also seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C3, but is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The source is most likely related to some field line movement seen beyond the NE limb best seen in GOES SUVI 284 beginning as early as 2026-05-24T18:30Z. The field line movement can also be faintly seen well beyond the E limb in STEREO A EUVI 195. There is also a narrow feature to the E in STEREO A COR2 that could be this CME, but a good triangulated fit cannot be made between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, particularly due to the grainy image quality in COR2A. No additional source was found.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2/C3.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and to the N/NW in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source of this CME is a filament eruption visible beyond the NE limb in GOES SUVI 304 and 284 starting around 2026-05-25T06:57Z. This eruption is also visible in SDO/AIA wavelengths.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap starting at 2026-05-25T20:53Z. Additionally, this event is blocked by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source is a far-sided eruption visible over the northeast limb in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195/284 imagery starting around 2026-05-25T20:09Z. A faint opening of field lines is observed over the northwest limb in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen STEREO A COR2 imagery due to the data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery starting at 2026-05-25T20:53Z. The source is an eruption visible near the northeast limb in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery starting around 2026-05-26T21:12Z. A subsequent opening of field lines is observed over the northeast limb in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195/284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measured longitude based on the approximate source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, the measured longitude may vary slightly.

cmes.note: Fast, halo CME first seen to the NW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-05-25T22:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is likely a far sided flare seen by the Solar orbiter STIX instrument peaking at approx. 2026-05-25T22:04Z with an estimated peak of M7.3 (potential range of [M4.0,X1.4]). Filament ejecta can also be seen in association with this eruption beyond the NW limb in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304 beginning at approx. 2026-05-25T21:57Z, along with wide opening field lines and EUV wave observed in SDO AIA 171, 193 and GOES SUVI 284. This event is also associated with solar energetic particle enhancements at SOHO, GOES and STEREO Ahead.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated BULK measurement based on SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Longitude and latitude were based on potential source signature in STEREO A EUVI 304 and 195 along the NW limb following the return of data around 2026-05-26T01:45Z.

Updated SHOCK measurement using potential source location from STEREO A EUVI 304/195. There is a higher level of uncertainty with this analysis due to limited triangulation ability as well as the unique shape of the CME front. This analysis tracks the furthest out portion of the shock (the "nose") of the CME and includes most of the shock that is more in the ecliptic plane, however this measurement also includes unavoidable empty/dead space due to the overall shape of the shock front and the restrictions of the SWPC_CAT measuring tool.
Preliminary leading edge analysis, assuming former AR 14436 (~N18W145 at this time). STEREO A COR2 observations not available due to data gap, and not yet observed in LASCO C3.

Shock front analysis with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. STEREO A COR2 not available due to a data gap. The shock front is notably irregular shaped, with a northern lobe and southern feature observed as a wide partial halo. This fit tracks the majority of the observed shock front prioritizing the northern lobe in similarity to the bulk leading edge directionality. Longitude of W145 used as the approximate source of former AR 14436; the likely source for this CME without direct observation, and as such longitude could vary between W135-W155.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not seen STEREO A COR2 imagery due to the data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery starting at 2026-05-25T20:53Z. The source is a far-sided eruption visible over/near the northeast limb in SDO AIA 131/171/193 and GOES SUVI 131/171/193/284 imagery starting around 2026-05-25T22:15Z. A subsequent opening of field lines is visible over the northeast limb in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/193/284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: Faint loop CME first seen to the NW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-05-25T23:24Z, as well by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 2026-05-25T20:53Z to 2026-05-26T01:23Z. The source of this event is likely continued far sided outflow following earlier CME: 2026-05-25T22:12Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and longitude fit of previous CME: 2026-05-25T22:12Z.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the NW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-05-26T00:48Z, as well by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This event is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 2026-05-25T20:53Z to 2026-05-26T01:23Z. The source of this event is likely continued far sided outflow following earlier CME: 2026-05-25T22:12Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit in GOES CCOR-1, SOHO LASCO C3 and longitude fit of earlier large CME: 2026-05-25T22:12Z.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-05-26T02:12Z, as well by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is likely continued far sided outflow following earlier CME: 2026-05-25T22:12Z and field line movement along the NW limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME visible to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, STEREO A COR2. There isn't a clear source, but there are moving/opening field lines visible in the SE as seen in SDO/AIA 193 around 2026-05-26T04:40Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the NW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-05-26T05:36Z, as well by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is likely far sided field line movement seen in SDO AIA 193 and continued outflow following earlier CME: 2026-05-25T22:12Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint, narrow CME first seen to the NW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-05-26T06:00Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO 3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is likely continued far sided outflow following earlier CME: 2026-05-25T22:12Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Bright streamer blowout CME to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This CME appears to be visibly wider in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 than in STEREO A COR2 imagery, which is likely due to the separate perspectives.

cmeAnalyses.note: Preliminary measurement using width of CME from STEREO A COR2's perspective. This measurement may be updated with later frames in SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: CME observed to the W in STEREO A COR2 only. This is likely far-sided, but no direct source is observed in available low-cadence STEREO A EUVI 195 and 304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A COR2 only plane-of-sky as the only observing coronagraph, and lack of observed source (likely far-sided from Earth). STEREO A currently at W58.8, putting its west plane-of-sky at W148.8 for this analysis.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SW in only a few frames of SOHO LASCO C2 before fading into background. The source is likely far-sided from Earth, and may be observed as slow field line movement beyond STEREO A's SW limb (limb ~W148.8 with STEREO A at W58.8) in STEREO A EUVI 195 starting as early as 2026-05-26T13:05Z and lasting several hours, past the CME start time.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only plane-of-sky as the only observing coronagraph, and likely but unclear far-sided source visible only as slow field line movement beyond STEREO A's SW limb. No STEREO A COR2 visibility of the front. Front fades into the running difference background after several frames.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NE only in SOHO LASCO C2, fading after a few frames. The source is on/just beyond the NE limb as filament ejecta and field line movement in GOES SUVI 304 starting at 2026-05-26T20:05Z. A second filament ejecta follows immediately after directed further northward, responsible for separate overlapping CME: 2026-05-26T21:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only 3D analysis, using E90 as the observed source longitude approximation and not as a plane-of-sky.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NE only in SOHO LASCO C2, fading after a few frames. The source is on/just beyond the NE limb as filament ejecta and field line movement in GOES SUVI 304 starting at 2026-05-26T21:09Z. This CME immediately follows and overlaps with CME: 2026-05-26T21:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2 only 3D analysis, using E90 as the observed source longitude approximation and not as a plane-of-sky.

cmes.note: Very faint partial halo CME observed in SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1, also in LASCO C3 obscured heavily by the pylon (and difficult to observe outside running difference imagery in all). STEREO A COR2 in a data gap at this time. The source may be some field line movement and breaking loops observed around 2026-05-26T21:39Z to the SE only in GOES SUVI 284, tracing to an approximate source on-disk around S30E53 (and could vary slightly, due to the brightness of this cluster of regions in SUVI 284).

cmeAnalyses.note: 3D SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 tracking using the approximate source ~E53 and using the partial halo shape for the width. Due to the faintness of this front and no COR2A to triangulate, the latitude, longitude, and width could vary.

cmes.note: This event is visible to the southwest in STEREO A COR2 imagery following a data gap between 2026-05-26T20:23Z to 2026-05-27T01:23Z. Due to the data gap, it is possible that the start time for this event is earlier than what is listed. This event is not seen in GOES CCOR-1 or in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery due to a real-time data gap between downlink periods. The source for this event is unknown and may therefore be far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of 148 degrees as the source may be far-sided and a source location is therefore unknown.

cmes.note: This event is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery. This event is not seen in STEREO A COR2 and is blocked by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source is an eruption centered near N17E90 starting around 2026-05-27T02:00Z which exhibits northern deflection as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery. A subsequent opening of field lines is visible over the northeast limb in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location. As this event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery and a triangulated measurement is not possible, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
